articles.title: Sleep Hygiene Basics for Anxious Minds
articles.summary: Practical steps to create a calmer nighttime routine and improve sleep when you live with anxiety.
articles.body: Good sleep hygiene is a set of habits that support deeper, more restorative rest. For people who experience anxiety, winding down at night can be especially challenging. In this article, we cover how to create a consistent bedtime routine, set up a sleep-friendly environment, limit stimulants and screen time, and respond skillfully when your mind starts racing as you try to fall asleep.

articles.title: Using CBT-I to Treat Chronic Insomnia
articles.summary: An overview of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) and what to expect from treatment.
articles.body: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) is an evidence-based treatment that targets the thoughts and behaviors that keep insomnia going. Sessions typically focus on understanding your sleep patterns, adjusting time in bed, and restructuring unhelpful beliefs about sleep. Unlike sleep medications, CBT-I aims to resolve the underlying problem so that improvements last long after treatment ends.

articles.title: How Evening Screen Time Impacts Your Sleep
articles.summary: Learn how blue light and late-night scrolling affect your ability to fall and stay asleep.
articles.body: Phones, tablets, and laptops emit blue light that signals to your brain that it is still daytime. This suppresses melatonin, the hormone that tells your body it is time to sleep. In addition, late-night scrolling often exposes you to emotionally activating content, making it harder to unwind. We outline realistic strategies to create healthier boundaries with technology without requiring a complete digital detox.

articles.title: Taming Racing Thoughts at Night
articles.summary: If your brain speeds up when your head hits the pillow, these strategies can help you slow it down.
articles.body: Racing thoughts at bedtime are a common experience for people dealing with anxiety and stress. Instead of trying to force your mind to be blank, it is often more effective to gently redirect your attention and give your brain a structured way to wind down. This article explores techniques like scheduled worry time, grounding exercises, and journaling prompts to help your mind shift into sleep mode.

articles.title: Breaking the Sleep–Stress Cycle
articles.summary: Chronic stress and poor sleep feed into each other. Here’s how to interrupt the cycle.
articles.body: When you are stressed, your body stays in a state of heightened arousal, which disrupts sleep. Poor sleep then makes it harder to regulate emotions and manage daily demands, increasing stress further. We describe this cycle in detail and offer small, realistic changes that can improve both stress levels and sleep quality over time.

articles.title: Understanding Anxiety: When Worry Becomes Too Much
articles.summary: Learn how to recognize the signs of anxiety and when it might be time to seek support.
articles.body: Anxiety is a normal human emotion, but when worry becomes persistent, intense, and hard to control, it can interfere with daily life. We review common symptoms of anxiety disorders, how they are diagnosed, and effective treatment options including CBT, exposure therapy, and medication. We also address common myths that prevent people from reaching out for help.

articles.title: Subtle Signs of Depression You Might Be Overlooking
articles.summary: Depression doesn’t always look like sadness. Here are less obvious signs to watch for.
articles.body: Many people imagine depression as constant sadness, but it often shows up as low motivation, irritability, or feeling emotionally numb. This article outlines common but easily missed signs of depression, how it can impact work, relationships, and physical health, and what to expect from reaching out to a therapist or psychiatrist.

articles.title: Communication Skills for Couples Under Stress
articles.summary: Practical tools for talking about hard topics without escalating conflict.
articles.body: Stressful seasons often magnify existing communication patterns between partners. We walk through specific tools used in couples therapy, including using "I" statements, taking structured breaks, and practicing reflective listening. These skills can help you stay connected even when you disagree or feel overwhelmed.

articles.title: How to Choose the Right Therapist for You
articles.summary: A step-by-step guide to finding a therapist whose style and expertise fit your needs.
articles.body: Finding a therapist can feel overwhelming. We suggest clarifying your goals, preferences, and practical constraints before you start searching. Then we outline how to read therapist profiles, what to ask in a consultation call, and how to evaluate fit after a few sessions. We also discuss common concerns about cost, insurance, and teletherapy.

articles.title: The Unique Benefits of Group Therapy
articles.summary: Why group therapy can be as powerful as individual work—and sometimes more so.
articles.body: Group therapy offers a space to practice new skills, receive feedback, and realize you are not alone in what you are facing. We describe different types of groups, who might benefit from them, and what a typical session in our stress management and anxiety skills groups looks like.

articles.title: Coping with Panic Attacks in the Moment
articles.summary: Step-by-step strategies you can use during a panic episode to feel safer and more in control.
articles.body: Panic attacks can feel frightening and unpredictable, but there are concrete tools that can help. We cover paced breathing, grounding, and cognitive strategies that many clients find helpful. We also discuss when it might be time to seek professional support for recurrent panic.

articles.title: Setting Boundaries to Manage Work Stress
articles.summary: How to create healthier limits around work so you have energy left for the rest of your life.
articles.body: Long hours, constant notifications, and unclear expectations can make work feel all-consuming. In this article, we explore practical boundary-setting strategies, communication tips, and mindset shifts that can reduce burnout risk and support better work-life balance.

articles.title: What Is Teletherapy and Is It Right for You?
articles.summary: An introduction to online therapy, including privacy, effectiveness, and what to expect.
articles.body: Teletherapy has made mental health care more accessible for many people. We review common platforms, how we protect your privacy, and research on the effectiveness of video sessions. We also discuss who might prefer in-person care and how to decide which format fits your needs and schedule.

articles.title: Grief and Healing: Moving at Your Own Pace
articles.summary: There is no "right" timeline for grief. Learn how to support yourself after loss.
articles.body: Grief can affect mood, concentration, sleep, and relationships. We describe common grief reactions, how to differentiate grief from clinical depression, and ways therapy can help you move through loss without rushing the process.

articles.title: Mindfulness Basics for Everyday Life
articles.summary: Simple mindfulness practices you can integrate into your routine in just a few minutes a day.
articles.body: Mindfulness is the practice of bringing nonjudgmental attention to the present moment. We share brief practices that can be woven into daily tasks, such as mindful walking, eating, and breathing exercises, and discuss how mindfulness can support anxiety and stress management.

articles.title: Draft: Advanced Strategies for Restless Sleepers
articles.summary: A work-in-progress guide to more advanced tools for persistent sleep difficulties.
articles.body: This draft article will cover advanced CBT-I strategies, stimulus control, and how to work with a therapist when basic sleep hygiene is not enough.

forms.name: Adult Intake
forms.description: Comprehensive intake questionnaire for new adult clients, including demographic information, presenting concerns, and symptom ratings.

forms.name: Consultation & Appointment Request
forms.description: General contact and consultation request form for new or returning clients.

forms.name: Group Registration Form
forms.description: Registration form for group therapy and workshop offerings, including stress management groups.

forms.name: Couples Therapy Inquiry
forms.description: Inquiry form for couples interested in starting counseling or scheduling a consultation.

locations.name: Downtown Office
locations.description: Our primary downtown office located near major bus and train lines, offering individual, couples, and group sessions.
locations.directions: Located on Market St between 12th and 13th. Use the main lobby entrance and take the elevator to the 5th floor. Paid garage parking is available next door.

locations.name: Midtown Office
locations.description: A quieter midtown office with convenient freeway access and reserved client parking behind the building.
locations.directions: From Highway 80, exit at Central Ave and head north. Turn right into the building’s private lot; client parking spaces are marked in blue.

locations.name: Westside Counseling Center
locations.description: A ground-floor, fully ADA-accessible office with ample free parking, ideal for in-person individual and couples sessions.
locations.directions: Located off Westlake Blvd with a large surface lot in front of the building. The counseling center entrance is on the east side, with an accessible ramp from the parking lot.

locations.name: Telehealth (Online Only)
locations.description: Online-only teletherapy sessions conducted via a secure, HIPAA-compliant video platform for clients located anywhere in the state.
locations.directions: You will receive a unique, secure video link before each session. Please join from a private, stable internet connection.

services.name: Individual Therapy
services.description: One-on-one psychotherapy focused on anxiety, depression, trauma, life transitions, and other concerns. Offered in-person and via secure teletherapy.

services.name: Couples Therapy
services.description: Relationship-focused therapy for couples seeking support with communication, conflict, intimacy, and life changes.

services.name: Group Therapy
services.description: Supportive therapy groups that combine psychoeducation, skills practice, and connection with others facing similar challenges.

services.name: Stress Management Group
services.description: A structured skills group that teaches practical tools for managing stress, burnout, and overwhelm in a supportive setting.

services.name: Family Therapy
services.description: Therapy sessions that involve multiple family members to address patterns of interaction, communication, and support.

services.name: Psychological Assessment
services.description: Comprehensive psychological and diagnostic assessments for clarity around learning, attention, mood, and personality concerns.

therapists.bio: Dr. Park specializes in anxiety, insomnia, and stress-related concerns. She integrates CBT, CBT-I, and mindfulness-based approaches to help clients build practical skills for calmer days and more restful nights.
therapists.experienceSummary: Dr. Park is a licensed psychologist with 9 years of experience supporting adults with anxiety, insomnia, and work-related stress.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Evening sessions available Mondays and Wednesdays until 7:30 PM.

therapists.bio: Dr. Rodriguez works with adults navigating depression, anxiety, and trauma, with a special focus on first-generation professionals and clients from Latinx communities. She offers therapy in both English and Spanish.
therapists.experienceSummary: Dr. Rodriguez is a bilingual psychologist with 11 years of experience providing individual and couples therapy to adults.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Offers evening appointments Tuesdays and Thursdays between 5:00 PM and 8:00 PM.

therapists.bio: Lisa is a therapist who helps clients manage chronic stress, anxiety, and sleep difficulties using CBT, DBT-informed skills, and practical coping strategies. She also facilitates our stress management groups.
therapists.experienceSummary: Lisa is an LCSW with 8 years of experience supporting adults dealing with anxiety, burnout, and insomnia.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Tuesday and Thursday evening sessions available from 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM.

therapists.credentials: LMFT, Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist
therapists.bio: Alex supports individuals, couples, and families working through grief, life transitions, and communication challenges. His style is warm, collaborative, and insight-oriented.
therapists.experienceSummary: Alex is an LMFT with 10 years of experience providing couples and family therapy.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Offers limited evening availability on Wednesdays from 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM.

therapists.credentials: LMFT, Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist
therapists.bio: Jordan specializes in couples therapy, helping partners improve communication, rebuild trust, and navigate major life changes. Jordan also works with individuals around relationships and attachment.
therapists.experienceSummary: Jordan is an LMFT with over 12 years of experience focusing on couples and relationship concerns.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Offers Monday and Thursday evening couples sessions between 5:00 PM and 9:00 PM.

therapists.bio: Megan helps clients manage work stress, burnout, and anxiety. She emphasizes practical boundary-setting, values clarification, and skills for sustainable change.
therapists.experienceSummary: Megan is an LPC with 7 years of experience working with professionals navigating stress, burnout, and anxiety.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Offers early evening appointments on Mondays and Wednesdays starting at 5:00 PM.

therapists.credentials: LMFT, Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist
therapists.bio: Carlos is a bilingual therapist who supports adults and couples facing depression, anxiety, and relationship stress. His approach combines CBT, emotion-focused work, and cultural humility.
therapists.experienceSummary: Carlos is a bilingual LMFT with 7 years of experience working with adults and couples in both English and Spanish.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Offers Spanish and English teletherapy sessions most weekdays between 5:30 PM and 8:30 PM.

therapists.bio: Ana offers teletherapy in English and Spanish for adults experiencing depression, trauma, and postpartum adjustments. She blends relational, trauma-informed, and CBT approaches.
therapists.experienceSummary: Ana is a bilingual LCSW with 6 years of experience providing online therapy to adults across the state.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Teletherapy appointments available Monday–Thursday evenings from 5:00 PM to 9:00 PM.

therapists.bio: Dr. Brooks specializes in therapy with adolescents and young adults, focusing on anxiety, ADHD, and school-related stress.
therapists.experienceSummary: Dr. Brooks is a psychologist with 5 years of experience working primarily with teens and college students.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Limited after-school and early evening appointments available on weekdays until 6:30 PM.

therapists.bio: Dr. Kim provides telehealth-based care for adults with anxiety, OCD, and ADHD using CBT and acceptance-based approaches.
therapists.experienceSummary: Dr. Kim is a psychologist with 9 years of experience offering exclusively online therapy for adults.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Evening teletherapy sessions available Tuesdays and Fridays from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM.

therapists.bio: Dr. Lee conducts psychological assessments for ADHD, learning differences, and mood concerns, and provides feedback sessions focused on clear next steps.
therapists.experienceSummary: Dr. Lee is a psychologist with 13 years of experience specializing in psychological and educational assessment.
therapists.eveningAvailabilityDescription: Assessment feedback sessions occasionally available on Thursday afternoons and early evenings.

groups.name: After-Work Stress Reset Group
groups.description: An in-person, skills-focused group for adults who feel overwhelmed by work and life stress. Each week includes brief education, guided practice, and time to apply tools to real-life situations.

groups.name: Online Stress Management Skills Group
groups.description: A teletherapy group that teaches CBT and mindfulness-based strategies for managing chronic stress, burnout, and anxiety. Participants join via secure video from home.

groups.name: Managing Stress in Demanding Careers
groups.description: An online group for professionals experiencing high work stress or burnout. Emphasis on boundary-setting, values, and sustainable habits.

groups.name: Anxiety Skills & Support Group
groups.description: A structured teletherapy group that combines CBT-based coping skills with peer support for adults living with generalized anxiety and panic.

groups.name: Mindful Sleep Workshop Series
groups.description: A 6-week workshop series combining CBT-I strategies with mindfulness practices to improve sleep quality and reduce nighttime anxiety.

groups.name: Saturday Depression Support Circle
groups.description: An in-person support group for adults experiencing mild to moderate depression, focusing on connection, coping skills, and behavioral activation.

groups.name: Teen DBT Skills Group
groups.description: A structured DBT skills group for teens focusing on emotion regulation, distress tolerance, and interpersonal effectiveness.
